content:
Yes, I am confident that the correct answer is (B) To measure galactic distances.

Cepheid variable stars are indeed used by astronomers as standard candles because of the reliable relationship between their pulsation period and intrinsic brightness. This allows astronomers to determine their absolute magnitude and, by comparing it with their apparent magnitude, calculate their distance. This method has been fundamental in measuring distances to nearby galaxies and improving our understanding of the size of the universe.

If you'd like, I can provide more details or references about Cepheid variables and their role in distance measurement.
